⏱️
Bug #01

Delay During Startup / Loading Sequence

📍 AreaApp Launch / Startup
🏷️ TypePerformance / UX
⚠️ SeverityMedium
🔁 FrequencyLow to Medium

📝 Description

When launching the game, the startup sequence includes a player intro animation along with a bat-hit sound effect. The sound design is a nice touch and adds to the feel of the game. However, the loading transition during this sequence takes slightly longer than expected, and at times it comes across as a lag before reaching the main screen.

✅ Expected Behaviour

The startup animation and transition should feel smooth and quick, without noticeable delay before entering the game.

❌ Actual Behaviour

There is a slight delay during the player intro/loading phase, which can make the transition feel slow or slightly laggy before reaching the home screen.

💡 Observation

The bat-hit sound effect during launch is a well-thought detail. The player intro concept is engaging, but optimizing the transition speed could greatly improve first impressions.

📸 Screenshots

🎥 Video Evidence

🏆
Bug #02

Failed to Initialize TrophyRoad

📍 AreaPvP Hub
🏷️ TypeFunctional / UX Issue
⚠️ SeverityMedium
🔁 FrequencyLow to Medium

📝 Description

While playing PvP matches, after completing a game (observed more consistently after winning), an error popup appears stating "Failed to Initialize TrophyRoad." Along with this, the UI elements related to progression (trophies/rewards) do not behave properly and at times appear to be misaligned or floating in inconsistent positions on the screen.

✅ Expected Behaviour

After completing a PvP match, the TrophyRoad/progression should load correctly, and UI elements should appear properly aligned without errors.

❌ Actual Behaviour

An error popup appears stating "Failed to Initialize TrophyRoad." Additionally, progression-related UI elements may not load correctly or appear visually inconsistent on screen.

💡 Observation

The TrophyRoad progression system is engaging and gives a clear sense of progression. The idea of placing Brilliant Player jerseys in the roadmap is one of the key aspects that made me come back and play more and build a stronger team.

📸 Screenshots

🎥 Video Evidence

🌡️
Bug #03

Device Heating During Extended Gameplay

📍 AreaGameplay (General – Across Sessions)
🏷️ TypePerformance / Device Behaviour
⚠️ SeverityMedium–High
🔁 FrequencyMedium

📝 Description

While playing the game over extended sessions, the device starts heating up noticeably. Initially the game feels stable, but over time (especially after longer play durations), the temperature increases to a point where the device feels genuinely hot rather than just warm. This is particularly noticeable around the camera region, making it slightly uncomfortable to hold.

✅ Expected Behaviour

During normal gameplay, the device temperature should remain within a comfortable range and not impact usability, even during longer sessions.

❌ Actual Behaviour

The device temperature increases over time, reaching around 39°C–42°C, making the device feel hot to touch especially near the camera area, and slightly affecting comfort during gameplay.

💡 Observation

The gameplay experience itself remains smooth initially, which shows the game handles performance well in shorter sessions. Even though the device heats up over time, there was no major drop in game performance.

📸 Screenshots

🎥 Video Evidence

🎮
Bug #04

60 FPS Not Fully Reflected / Lag on Opponent Quit

📍 AreaHome Screen / PvP Gameplay / In-Game Settings
🏷️ TypePerformance / Gameplay Experience
⚠️ SeverityMedium
🔁 FrequencyMedium

📝 Description

The game includes a 60 FPS option in settings which was enabled during testing. While checking via device game tools, the non-gameplay home screen ran at ~50 FPS. However, during actual PvP gameplay, the frame rate stays closer to ~30 FPS on average. This becomes more noticeable when the opponent quits/disconnects, where a visible lag or pause occurs.

✅ Expected Behaviour

With 60 FPS enabled, both lighter screens and gameplay should maintain smoother and more consistent frame output, especially during active match scenarios, even when the opponent quits.

❌ Actual Behaviour

The home screen appears to run at ~50 FPS, but gameplay runs closer to ~30 FPS on average. A noticeable lag/pause is also observed when the opponent quits, affecting the smoothness of the PvP experience.

💡 Observation

The presence of a 60 FPS option, quality controls, and touch response settings is a strong customization feature. If the same smoothness from the home screen carries into gameplay and quit/disconnect moments, the PvP experience would feel much cleaner.

📸 Screenshots

🎥 Video Evidence

🔔
Bug #05

Notification Indicator Without Clear Context

📍 AreaHome Screen / Events / Daily Co-op Quest
🏷️ TypeUX / UI Observation
⚠️ SeverityLow–Medium
🔁 FrequencyHigh

📝 Description

On the home screen, a red notification/alert indicator appears near the Daily Co-op Quest / Events area, which naturally gives the impression that there is something pending or requiring attention. However, even after opening and checking the related section, there does not appear to be any clear update, reward, task, or specific actionable item tied to that alert.

✅ Expected Behaviour

If a notification/alert symbol is shown, it should clearly point toward a new, pending, or actionable item that actually needs the player attention.

❌ Actual Behaviour

A notification indicator is visible, but after checking the related section, there is no clear or immediate reason for the alert, which makes it feel unnecessary or unclear.

💡 Observation

The use of notification cues is good in general, as it helps draw attention toward active areas and can improve engagement when used meaningfully. If the alert is tied more clearly to a visible task, reward, or update, it would make the interaction feel more intentional.

📸 Screenshots

🎥 Video Evidence

💬
Bug #06

Alliance Chat / Reconnect Not Functioning Properly

📍 AreaAlliance Hub / Chat Section
🏷️ TypeFunctional / Network / UX Issue
⚠️ SeverityMedium
🔁 FrequencyLow

📝 Description

While navigating to the Alliance section, the chat area does not load properly and remains blank. Instead of showing messages or chat activity, a "Retrying…" state is displayed continuously along with a Reconnect button. Even after attempting to reconnect, the chat does not always recover immediately, and no messages or interaction appear in the chat area. This makes the chat feature unusable during that session and gives the impression that the feature is not functioning properly.

✅ Expected Behaviour

The alliance chat should load properly and display messages without requiring repeated reconnect attempts. If a network issue occurs, reconnect should restore the chat quickly and reliably.

❌ Actual Behaviour

The chat section remains blank and shows a "Retrying…" state. Even after attempting to reconnect, the chat does not consistently load, making the feature unusable.

💡 Observation

The Alliance feature itself feels interactive and well-designed with multiple entry points like chat, co-op quest, and academy. If the chat flow becomes stable and loads reliably, it would make the alliance experience feel much more active and engaging.
